title: ChartZoom Modal — Recharts Blank Canvas on Zoom (Recurrence)
root_cause: ResponsiveContainer (Recharts) uses DOM.offsetHeight to measure its parent container and determine render dimensions. The modal chart container used Tailwind CSS classes (h-[70vh], flex-1, min-h-[400px]) instead of explicit inline style height. During the 200ms CSS animation (chartZoomIn scale transition), the flex layout was not fully resolved, so offsetHeight returned 0 or incorrect values. Recharts rendered with height=0 → blank canvas. Additionally, the chart mounted synchronously with the modal open, racing against the animation/layout settle.
resolution: Three-layer fix applied to ChartZoom.tsx: (1) Modal container uses inline style height='88vh' instead of CSS flex classes — gives Recharts a concrete pixel value. (2) Chart area uses inline style height='calc(88vh - 73px)' — explicit, not flex-derived. (3) Added useEffect modalMounted flag with 50ms delay — chart only mounts after DOM is settled, showing a spinner meanwhile. Added key={title} prop to force fresh Recharts instance instead of reusing the background chart's DOM.
prevention: RULE: When using Recharts ResponsiveContainer inside CSS animations or flex containers, ALWAYS provide explicit pixel height via inline style. Never rely on flex-1 or h-[vh] CSS classes for ResponsiveContainer measurement. The 50ms mounted delay pattern should be reused whenever Recharts mounts inside a modal/dialog with opening animations.
blast_radius: MEDIUM — affects all 20 chart instances across Dashboard, Finance, and Reports modules when zoomed.
